query: STF Marco Civil da Internet embargos de declaracao RE 1037396 Tema 987 June 2026 status voting results proclamation
answer:
The STF began judging embargos de declaracao (motions for clarification) against the thesis fixed in the Marco Civil da Internet case (RE 1037396 / Tema 987) on June 10-11, 2026, in physical plenary session. The Plenario concluded voting on nine of twelve resources from big techs and civil society entities, but the formal proclamation of the result was postponed to Wednesday June 17, 2026 for drafting adjustments. Three additional resources remain to be analyzed on that same date.

Key outcomes from the June 10-11 session:

1. MAJORITY FOR 60-DAY ADAPTATION PERIOD: The STF formed a majority backing relator Min. Dias Toffoli's proposal to grant platforms 60 days to adapt to the new obligations imposed by the June 2025 ruling.

2. TOFFOLI LIMITING SCOPE TO BIG TECHS: Toffoli proposed that the heaviest structural obligations (specific customer service channels, transparency reports) apply only to large-scale providers, defined as those with over 1 million users in Brazil.

3. DIVERGENCE ON SOLIDARY LIABILITY: Min. Andre Mendonca opened divergence on the solidary (joint) liability for platforms after extrajudicial notification. He argued presumed solidarity could create a 'chilling effect' causing platforms to preemptively censor user speech. Min. Nunes Marques shared this concern.

4. DIVERGENCE ON RETROACTIVITY: A bloc of four ministers (Dino, Mendonca, Nunes Marques, Fux) opposed retroactive application of the thesis to cases filed before June 26, 2025 (the original merit judgment date). Toffoli had proposed retroactivity to that date.

5. DINO VS TOFFOLI ON SCOPE: Min. Flavio Dino proposed removing the 1-million-user threshold, arguing all providers should comply. Mendonca opposed, arguing this would stifle Brazilian startups.

6. FACHIN 'SAFE HARBOR' PROPOSAL: President Min. Edson Fachin proposed a safe harbor: platforms would be exempt from punishment if they demonstrate diligent analysis but maintained a post due to 'reasonable doubt' about its illegality.

The proclamation of results was postponed to June 17, 2026, for drafting adjustments to reconcile the various positions. The STF will also analyze three remaining resources on that date.

BACKGROUND: In June 2025, the STF ruled 8-3 to declare partial unconstitutionality of Article 19 of the Marco Civil da Internet, which had required a prior court order before platforms could be held liable for user content. The new interpretation imposed greater proactive duties on platforms. The embargos de declaracao were filed by tech companies (Google, Facebook) and civil society entities seeking clarification of the thesis.

sources.title: STF se divide sobre responsabilizacao e vigencia de tese para big techs
sources.snippet: Divergencias entre os ministros do STF adiaram para a proxima quarta-feira (17) a conclusao do julgamento de nove recursos apresentados por big techs e entidades do setor de tecnologia contra a nova interpretacao do Marco Civil da Internet. O principal impasse envolve o alcance das obrigacoes impostas as plataformas e a data de entrada em vigor das novas regras.

sources.title: STF tem maioria pelo prazo de 60 dias para plataformas se adequarem a tese do Marco Civil
sources.snippet: Julgamento ocorreu no ambito de recursos contra a tese sobre responsabilizacao de plataformas por conteudo de usuarios. Published 2026-06-12. Author: Karla Gamba.

sources.title: Toffoli propoe limitar responsabilidade sobre circulacao de ilicitos a big techs

key_facts: STF began judging embargos de declaracao on RE 1037396 / Tema 987 (Marco Civil da Internet, Article 19) on June 10, 2026 in physical plenary session
Nine of twelve embargos resources were voted on during sessions of June 10-11, 2026; three remain pending
Formal proclamation of the result was postponed to Wednesday June 17, 2026, for drafting adjustments to reconcile divergent positions
STF formed majority for 60-day adaptation period for platforms to comply with new obligations (following relator Toffoli's proposal)
Relator Min. Dias Toffoli proposed limiting heaviest structural obligations to 'big tech' providers with over 1 million users in Brazil
Min. Flavio Dino diverged from Toffoli, proposing to remove the 1-million-user threshold so all providers must comply
Min. Andre Mendonca opened divergence on solidary (joint) liability after extrajudicial notification, warning of 'chilling effect' on speech
Min. Nunes Marques also expressed concern about conflating platform negligence with user intent (dolo)
A bloc of 4 ministers (Dino, Mendonca, Nunes Marques, Fux) opposed retroactive application of the thesis to pre-June 26, 2025 cases
Min. Luiz Fux stated 'Jurisprudence, like law, is a formal source of Law and cannot be applied retroactively'
President Min. Edson Fachin proposed a safe harbor: platforms exempt from punishment if they demonstrate diligent analysis despite maintaining post due to 'reasonable doubt'
Original June 2025 merit judgment was 8-3 to declare partial unconstitutionality of Article 19 of the Marco Civil da Internet
Embargos were filed by Google, Facebook, and civil society entities questioning: absence of adaptation deadline, scope of platform obligations, and civil liability criteria
The case was moved from virtual to physical plenary around May 29, 2026 (per Estadao)
US pressure on Brazil regarding big tech regulation was noted as contextual factor (per GZH/RBS coverage)

data_points.metric: Original merit judgment vote count (unconstitutionality of Art. 19 Marco Civil)
Toffoli proposed big tech threshold (users in Brazil)
Total embargos de declaracao resources filed
